flags.concern: video-editor-effect-registry: Step 2's code-only dedup would freeze or race parameter schema updates when code stays the same.
flags.evidence: `useEffectRegistry()` re-registers resource effects with both `effect.code` and `effect.parameterSchema` (`src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useEffectRegistry.ts:52`), and `EffectCreatorPanel` persists `parameterSchema` independently of `code` when editing an effect (`src/tools/video-editor/components/EffectCreatorPanel.tsx:368-375`, `:381-384`). In `DynamicEffectRegistry.registerAsync()`, the current `pending.schema !== schema` check at `src/tools/video-editor/effects/DynamicEffectRegistry.ts:59-64` is what prevents an older async compile from overwriting a newer same-code/different-schema registration. Removing schema from the early-return and stale-pending checks would leave the old schema in place when only the schema changes, and can let the wrong schema win if two same-code async registrations race.

flags.concern: video-editor-effect-registry: Recurring debt: Step 3's identity guard still mutates the module registry during React render, so it does not actually contain the render-purity bug it is targeting.
flags.evidence: `useEffectRegistry()` currently calls `replaceEffectRegistry(registry)` directly in render (`src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useEffectRegistry.ts:27-30`). The proposed guard still has to call `getEffectRegistry()` during render, which lazily allocates the singleton when empty (`src/tools/video-editor/effects/index.tsx:82-87`), and it still performs `replaceEffectRegistry()` during render whenever the instance differs (`src/tools/video-editor/effects/index.tsx:90-92`). The existing test the plan cites only checks that children see the same registry instance (`src/tools/video-editor/effects/DynamicEffectRegistry.test.tsx:232-255`); it does not verify the new 'not on every render' requirement or catch the remaining render-time side effect on initial mount.
